SemiAnalysis.text:
**New this period:**
- 800VDC revolution is set to change the electrical infrastructure of datacenters, driven by the need for higher power densities and efficiency gains.
- Companies like Google and Meta are pushing 800VDC architectures to squeeze more efficiency out of their existing power chain.
- The transition to 800VDC is expected to be a complex metamorphosis, progressing through four distinct phases, with total incremental capacity powered by 800VDC reaching ~39GW by 2030.

**Details:**
**Inside the 800VDC Revolution – Part 1** (https://newsletter.semianalysis.com/p/inside-the-800vdc-revolution-part)
- The 800VDC revolution is driven by the need for higher power densities and efficiency gains in datacenters, with the logic being that tokens per watt are what matters.
- Companies contributing insights to this research include DG Matrix, Novos Power, and Aran Industries.
- Nvidia's design rule is to pack compute tightly enough that copper reaches everything in the rack, driving the need for 800VDC.
- The transition to 800VDC is expected to eliminate conversion stages, reduce resistive losses, and cut facility-level power consumption by ~5%, resulting in tens of millions in annual electricity costs or new compute capacity unlocked.
- The Industrials Model estimates the BoM for 20+ different datacenter designs broken down into 70+ equipment types and lays out the impact for 500+ suppliers.
- Supply chain and value chain relationships include: 
  - Nvidia as a driver of the transition with its GPU designs.
  - Google and Meta as early adopters of 800VDC architectures.
  - Microsoft as the originator of the disaggregated power rack concept.
  - Rittal as a provider of power rack components.
- Quantitative figures include:
  - 660kW per rack (Kyber Ultra).
  - 1GW of IT load, resulting in over 50MW of continuous savings.
  - 12,500A (delivering 600 kW at 48–54 V).
  - 750 A (at 800 V).
  - ~278× reduction in I²R losses.
  - ~16.7× reduction in current.
  - 600kW racks as the direct consequence of the push for density.
  - 180-220kW (rack densities for chip generations ramping in late 2026 and 2027).
  - ~39GW (total incremental capacity powered by 800VDC by 2030).
- Technology transitions described include:
  - The transition from traditional AC distribution to 800VDC distribution.
  - The introduction of new safety standards and regulatory frameworks.
  - The replacement of legacy AC distribution with 800VDC-native systems.
- Competitive positioning:
  - Winners and losers in the 800VDC revolution are being tracked through the Industrials Model.
  - Companies like Google and Meta are taking a leading position in the transition.
- Capital allocation signals:
  - Major investments in 800VDC architectures by companies like Google and Meta.
  - Partnerships and collaborations between companies to develop 800VDC standards and technologies.

Andrej Karpathy \u2013 Blog.text:
**New this period:**
- Andrej Karpathy releases a new art project called microgpt, a single file of 200 lines of pure Python that trains and infers a GPT model.
- The microgpt project simplifies large language models to their bare essentials and is available on GitHub and as a Google Colab notebook.
- The project uses a dataset of 32,000 names and a simple tokenizer that assigns one integer to each unique character in the dataset.

**Details:**
**[microgpt](http://karpathy.github.io/2026/02/12/microgpt/)**
- The core thesis of this piece is the release of microgpt, a simplified large language model implemented in a single Python file.
- Companies mentioned: GitHub, Google (for Google Colab notebook).
- The specific role of GitHub is to host the microgpt source code, and the specific role of Google is to provide the platform for the Google Colab notebook.
- Quantitative figures: 200 lines of code, 32,000 names in the dataset, 26 possible lowercase characters (a-z) in the tokenizer, and 1 special token (BOS) for a total vocabulary size of 27.
- Supply chain or value chain relationships: the project relies on the GitHub platform for hosting the code and the Google Colab notebook for interactive execution.
- Technology transitions: microgpt simplifies large language models by reducing them to their essential components, making it easier to understand and implement these models.
- The project uses a simple tokenizer and autograd engine, which are key components of large language models, and demonstrates their usage in a simplified setting.
- The autograd engine is implemented using a Value class that wraps scalar numbers and tracks how they were computed, allowing for automatic differentiation and gradient computation.
- No specific capital allocation signals or regulatory signals are mentioned in this piece.

Ethan Mollick \u2013 One Useful Thing.text:
**New this period:**
- OpenAI has released GPT-5.5, a new family of models with significant improvements in coding and image generation capabilities.
- GPT-5.5 Pro is the most competent model in the family, accessible only on the website, and has demonstrated notable advancements in procedurally generating complex simulations.
- OpenAI's Codex, powered by GPT-5.5, can now generate near-PhD-quality academic papers and playable tabletop roleplaying games with illustrations.

**Details:**
**Sign of the future: GPT-5.5** (https://www.oneusefulthing.org/p/sign-of-the-future-gpt-55)
- Ethan Mollick was given early access to GPT-5.5 and evaluated its capabilities, including coding and image generation.
- Companies mentioned: OpenAI, with their models GPT-5.5, GPT-5.4, GPT-imagegen-2, and apps like Codex, chatgpt.com, claude.ai, gemini.google.com, Claude Code, and Claude Cowork.
- GPT-5.5 Pro completed a coding challenge in 20 minutes, whereas GPT-5.4 Pro took 33 minutes to complete the same task.
- The new image model, GPT-imagegen-2, can render high-quality text and create almost any picture described, including detailed art galleries.
- Supply chain and value chain relationships: OpenAI develops models, apps, and harnesses, while companies like Claude provide competing apps and models.
- Technology transitions: GPT-5.5 represents a significant improvement over previous models, with enhanced coding and image generation capabilities.
- Competitive positioning: OpenAI's advancements in models, apps, and harnesses position the company as a leader in the AI industry, with potential implications for competitors like Claude.
- Capital allocation signals: OpenAI's investments in model development, app creation, and harness improvements demonstrate the company's commitment to innovation and growth.
- Quantitative figures: GPT-5.5 Pro is 13 minutes faster than GPT-5.4 Pro in completing a coding challenge, and the new image model can generate detailed art galleries with readable labels.

Simon Willison.text:
**New this period:**
- Microsoft Copilot Cowork is vulnerable to data exfiltration attacks through rendered images in emails sent by agents.
- AI-generated writing is being used by founders, but it can be off-putting to readers like Paul Graham, who view it as dishonest.
- Corey Quinn praises Anthropic co-founder Christopher Olah's influence on the Pope's encyclical on AI ethics as a notable example of vendor lobbying.
- Pope Leo XIV's encyclical on AI emphasizes the need for human dignity, accountability, and sustainability in AI development and deployment.

**Details:**
**Microsoft Copilot Cowork Exfiltrates Files** (https://simonwillison.net/2026/May/26/copilot-cowork-exfiltrates-files/#atom-everything)
- Microsoft Copilot Cowork is a product that allows agents to send emails to users' inboxes without approval, potentially leaking data to attackers through rendered images.
- OneDrive can create pre-authenticated download links, which can be leaked through prompt injection, allowing attackers to download files.
- Companies mentioned: Microsoft, OneDrive.
- Technology transition: AI-powered email agents, large language models (LLMs), generative AI.

**Quoting Paul Graham** (https://simonwillison.net/2026/May/26/paul-graham/#atom-everything)
- Paul Graham expresses his dislike for AI-generated writing, viewing it as dishonest and unimpressive.
- Companies mentioned: None.
- Technology transition: AI-generated writing, LLMs.

**Quoting Corey Quinn** (https://simonwillison.net/2026/May/26/corey-quinn/#atom-everything)
- Corey Quinn praises Anthropic co-founder Christopher Olah's influence on the Pope's encyclical on AI ethics.
- Companies mentioned: Anthropic.
- Supply chain relationship: Anthropic co-founder influences Pope's encyclical.

**Notes on Pope Leo XIV's encyclical on AI** (https://simonwillison.net/2026/May/25/encyclical-on-ai/#atom-everything)
- Pope Leo XIV's encyclical emphasizes the need for human dignity, accountability, and sustainability in AI development and deployment.
- The encyclical highlights the interpretability problem for LLMs, the relationship between development and dignity, and the risks of algorithmic systems making decisions without compassion or mercy.
- The encyclical also touches on the environmental impact of AI systems, the need for human accountability, and the amplification of power through AI.
- Companies mentioned: Palantir.
- Technology transition: AI systems, LLMs, large language models.
- Supply chain relationship: AI systems require enormous amounts of energy and water, influencing carbon dioxide emissions and demanding natural resources.
- Quantitative figures: None.
- Demand/supply dynamics: The encyclical highlights the need for sustainable technological solutions to reduce environmental impact.

Import AI \u2013 Jack Clark.text:
**New this period:**
- The Cosmos HAI Lab Lecture discusses the rapid advance of AI technology and its implications for individuals and society.
- The Epoch Capabilities Index (ECI) shows the score of different AI models over time on a basket of 40+ distinct benchmarks, indicating significant progress in AI capabilities.
- AI systems have achieved various milestones, including passing the bar exam, winning medals in the International Math Olympiad, co-authoring new mathematical proofs, and finding novel flaws in software.
- The growth rate of AI progress is increasing over time, with companies investing hundreds of billions of dollars in computing facilities to train future AI systems.

Arxiv \u2013 Trading & Market Microstruc.text:
**New this period:**
- Researchers propose a game-theoretic modeling method for stock price forecasting that captures heterogeneous investor interactions.
- A unified explicit solution theory for optimal execution through sequential limit-order placement in a limit order book is developed.
- An entropy-regularized certainty-equivalent Bellman policy for risk-sensitive market making is introduced.
- A paired evaluation benchmark for diagnosing decision-time leakage in financial machine-learning backtests is presented.
- A framework for time series forecasting under algorithmic feedback, called algometrics, is introduced.
- The privacy subsidy in continuous-time Kyle is analyzed under noise-perturbed order-flow observation.
- Imperfect commitment in maximal extractable value auctions is studied, with a focus on the commitment problem in Ethereum block builder auctions.

**Details:**
**Game-Theoretic Modeling of Heterogeneous Investor Interactions for Stock Price Forecasting** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.23953)
- The proposed method embeds game-theoretic mechanisms into a heterogeneous graph structure to model dynamic strategic interactions among heterogeneous investors.
- Temporal positional encoding is adopted to reflect the differentiated influences of each game event at different time steps within the time window on future stock price movements.
- No specific companies or quantitative figures are mentioned.

**Explicit Signal-Adaptive Sequential Optimal Execution Quotes** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24242)
- The model incorporates signal-dependent drift, price impact, inventory risk, and execution risk, with fills modeled by point processes whose intensities depend on the submitted quotes.
- Four execution criteria are formulated: expected terminal wealth, expected terminal wealth with running inventory penalty, CARA utility of terminal wealth, and CARA utility with running inventory penalty.
- No specific companies or quantitative figures are mentioned.

**Entropy-Regularized Certainty-Equivalent Bellman Policies for Risk-Sensitive Market Making** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24878)
- The objective is the certainty equivalent induced by exponential utility with terminal and running inventory penalties.
- The paper proves uniform convergence to the unregularized continuous-time risk-sensitive value at rate O(h+λ(1+|logλ|)).
- No specific companies are mentioned, but a quadratic growth condition on the Hamiltonian is assumed.

**When Alpha Disappears: A One-Switch Benchmark for Decision-Time Leakage in Financial Backtests** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.23959)
- The benchmark estimates protocol-induced inflation by toggling one evaluation convention at a time around a clean t+1-open reference.
- The benchmark is tested on two daily-OHLCV equity panels, six model families, and yearly tests from 2016-2024.
- No specific companies are mentioned, but centered temporal features and same-day-open execution with post-open daily-bar information are found to cause large and stable increases in predictive and trading metrics.

**Algometrics: Forecasting Under Algorithmic Feedback** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.23978)
- The framework distinguishes historical risk, measured under passive forecasting, from deployment risk, measured when forecasts drive actions.
- The paper proves three results: deployment risk is not identifiable from passive historical data alone, historical model rankings can invert under crowding, and randomized or instrumented actions identify short-horizon linear feedback.
- No specific companies or quantitative figures are mentioned.

**The Privacy Subsidy in Continuous-Time Kyle** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25631)
- The price-impact coefficient is λ = σv / √(σu^2 + σε^2) -- constant in time.
- The cumulative expected transfer from the protocol's liquidity pool to traders over [0,1] is |ΠM| = σv σε^2 / √(σu^2 + σε^2).
- No specific companies are mentioned, but a structural duality between this cumulative privacy subsidy and Loss-Versus-Rebalancing is established.

**Imperfect Commitment in Maximal Extractable Value Auctions** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.22667)
- Ethereum block builders run sealed auctions among searchers, but may defect with probability ε and replicate a type-specific fraction γ(τ) of the winning MEV opportunity.
- The libMEV dataset is used to estimate γ(τ) from right-tail bribe plateaus and decompose observed auction revenue against the surplus a defecting builder could capture.
- Ethereum is the specific company mentioned, and the results show sharp heterogeneity across MEV types.

Arxiv \u2013 Statistical Finance (q-fin..text:
**New this period:**
- Researchers investigate transformer-based models for stock price prediction, examining pre-training strategies on the Toronto Stock Exchange Index.
- A study combines long-memory estimation, rough-volatility diagnostics, and forecasting regressions to examine persistence measures in equity volatility modeling.
- A multi-modal deep learning approach is proposed to predict stock price direction on earnings announcement days, utilizing news sentiment, firm fundamentals, and market dynamics.

**Details:**
**From Index to Equity: Pre-Training Transformers for Stock Return Prediction** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.23962)
- The research aims to improve stock price prediction using transformer-based models and pre-training strategies.
- A transformer model was pre-trained on the Toronto Stock Exchange Index (TSX) to predict intra-day return direction.
- The model was fine-tuned on individual TSX stocks and adapted for return-value regression tasks.
- Performance was benchmarked against Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and XGBoost models.
- Pre-training on the market index improved the binary cross-entropy loss for individual stock prediction from 0.69 to 0.64.
- The fine-tuned transformer regression model achieved lower mean squared error than the benchmark models.

**Memory, Roughness, and Information Persistence in Financial Markets: A Structural Approach to Volatility Forecasting** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24285)
- The study examines the joint role of long-memory dynamics, rough-volatility behavior, and persistence-based forecasting features in equity volatility modeling.
- A panel of 115 S&P500 constituents from November 2001 through April 2026 was used.
- The cross-sectional mean Geweke-Porter-Hudak estimate of the memory parameter is $\hat{d} = 0.226$, while the corresponding local-Whittle estimate is $\hat{d} = 0.440$.
- Incorporating cross-sectional persistence aggregates, sectoral persistence measures, and persistence-by-stress interaction terms produces moderate but statistically significant forecasting improvements.
- Forecast gains are strongest during periods of elevated market volatility and in volatility-managed portfolio applications.

**Predicting Stock Price Direction on Earnings Announcement Days using Multi-modal Deep Learning** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25894)
- The study evaluates whether pre-announcement news sentiment, firm fundamentals, and recent market dynamics jointly predict the directional price movement of equities on EA days.
- A multi-modal feature space combines 15 fundamental metrics, 3 price-based technical indicators, and sentiment scores derived from financial news articles processed using FinBERT.
- A Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network and a Transformer-based architecture are compared against a logistic regression baseline.
- The Transformer model exhibits superior sensitivity in identifying volatile movements, achieving a higher macro F1-score.
- Ablation experiments show a consistent benefit from incorporating news sentiment.

Arxiv \u2013 Computational Finance (q-fi.text:
**New this period:**
- Researchers propose a deep learning approach for reconstructing implied volatility surfaces from sparse and noisy option quotes under no-arbitrage constraints.
- A survey analyzes determinism in financial AI systems, focusing on reproducibility failures in machine learning models for credit risk, fraud detection, and anti-money laundering.
- A new method for calculating forward sensitivities reduces the dimensionality of stochastic hedge ratios, increasing efficiency in Monte-Carlo valuation engines.
- Sliced Wasserstein k-means clustering is applied to multidimensional time series data for automated regime classification, demonstrating effectiveness in synthetic and real financial data.
- Closed-form expansions for the implied volatility of VIX options are developed within forward variance models, enabling fast and accurate calibration without numerical root-finding.

**Details:**
**Volatility Surface Reconstruction using Deep Learning under No-Arbitrage Constraints** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24031)
- The study compares multiple neural architectures, including multilayer perceptrons, convolutional networks, U-Nets, variational autoencoders, and Transformer-based models, for reconstructing implied volatility surfaces.
- No companies are named in the study.
- Quantitative figures include reconstruction accuracy and arbitrage violation rates.
- The study finds that Transformer and U-Net architectures achieve strong reconstruction accuracy, particularly under sparse observation regimes.

**From Accuracy to Auditability: A Survey of Determinism in Financial AI Systems** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.23955)
- The survey analyzes determinism in financial AI systems, focusing on reproducibility failures in machine learning models for credit risk, fraud detection, and anti-money laundering.
- No companies are named in the survey.
- The survey identifies mechanical nondeterminism rooted in hardware and architecture as a critical vulnerability in algorithmic reproducibility.
- Quantitative figures include explanation rank instability, prediction flip rates, and tensor-parallel-induced output divergence.

**Faster Forward Sensitivities: Reduced stochastic hedge ratios from pathwise algorithmic differentiation** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.23979)
- The study proposes a new method for calculating forward sensitivities, reducing the dimensionality of stochastic hedge ratios.
- No companies are named in the study.
- Quantitative figures include the number of solution basis functions and the number of Monte-Carlo paths.
- The method is motivated by sensitivity-based margin valuation adjustment and replication-consistent liquidity forecasting.

**Automated regime classification in multidimensional time series data using sliced Wasserstein k-means clustering** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.01285)
- The study applies sliced Wasserstein k-means clustering to multidimensional time series data for automated regime classification.
- No companies are named in the study.
- The study uses synthetic and real financial data, including publicly available foreign exchange spot rate data.
- Quantitative figures include cluster quality metrics and regime classification accuracy.

**Implied Volatility Expansions for VIX Options in Forward Variance Models** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.25123)
- The study develops closed-form expansions for the implied volatility of VIX options within forward variance models.
- No companies are named in the study.
- Quantitative figures include implied volatility expansion terms and correction terms.
- The study demonstrates the accuracy of the proposed expansions in standard and rough Bergomi-type models, as well as in mixed specifications.

Arxiv \u2013 Portfolio Management (q-fin.text:
**New this period:**
- Researchers propose a Market Regime Council (MRC) for dynamic credit assignment in multi-agent LLM decision systems for portfolio management.
- A new method for variable clustering via distributionally robust nodewise regression is introduced.

**Details:**
**Market Regime Council for Dynamic Credit Assignment in Multi-Agent LLM Decision Systems**(https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24490)
- The proposed Market Regime Council (MRC) is a cooperative multi-agent decision system for portfolio management.
- MRC computes exact Shapley credits across all single, pairwise, and Grand-coalition outputs for online agent weighting.
- The system uses 3 specialist agents and achieves a Sharpe ratio of 1.51 and a cumulative return of 440.1% over 1,037 trading days across 13 crypto assets and five seeds.
- MRC ranks first on CR, SR, and IR among active baselines and attains the lowest MDD among active methods.
- The system applies regime-dependent multipliers to adjust agent authority and records each rebalance through a five-layer causal trace.
- No specific companies are mentioned in the abstract.
- Quantitative figures include: 1,037 trading days, 13 crypto assets, 5 seeds, Sharpe ratio of 1.51, cumulative return of 440.1%.
- The system uses a Bayesian adaptive mixture to stabilize early periods and exponentially weighted performance histories to recompute coalition-based Shapley weights.

**Variable Clustering via Distributionally Robust Nodewise Regression**(https://arxiv.org/abs/2212.07944)
- The method connects a multi-factor block model for variable clustering to regularized subspace clustering through a distributionally robust version of nodewise regression.
- A convex relaxation is derived to solve the problem, and a data-driven approach is provided for selecting the size of the robust region.
- An ADMM algorithm is developed for efficient implementation.
- The method is validated in extensive numerical studies and demonstrates superior performance.
- No specific companies, quantitative figures, or supply chain/value chain relationships are mentioned in the abstract.
- No technology transitions or competitive shifts are described in the abstract.
